ait: noun. An island in a river, especially the River Thames in England.
apt: adjective. Suitable; appropriate; fit or fitted; suited.
cap: noun. A close-fitting hat, either brimless or peaked.
capacity: noun. The ability to hold, receive, or absorb.
cat: noun. A mammal of the family Felidae.
cay: noun. A small, low island largely made of sand or coral.
city: noun. A large settlement, bigger than a town; sometimes with a specific legal definition, depending on the place.
icy: adjective. Pertaining to, resembling, or abounding in ice; cold; frosty.
paca: noun. Any of the large rodents of the genus Cuniculus (but see also its synonyms), native to Central America and South America, which have
pact: noun. An agreement; a compact; a covenant.
pat: noun. The sound of a light slap or tap with a soft flat object, especially of a footstep.
pay: verb. To give money or other compensation to in exchange for goods or services.
pia: noun. The pia mater, the innermost of the meninges that protect the brain and spinal cord.
pic: noun. A picture, especially a photographic image.
pica: noun. A disorder characterized by appetite and craving for non-edible substances, such as chalk, clay, dirt, ice, or sand.
pit: noun. A hole in the ground.
pita: noun. A piece of pita bread; a flatbread pouch used for making sandwiches such as gyros or falafels.
pity: noun. A feeling of sympathy at the misfortune or suffering of someone or something.
pya: noun. A subdivision of currency, equal to one hundredth of a Burmese kyat.
tap: noun. A conical peg or pin used to close and open the hole or vent in a container.
tapa: noun. A kind of cloth prepared by the Polynesians from the inner bark of the paper mulberry.
tic: noun. A sudden, nonrhythmic motor movement or vocalization.
tip: noun. The extreme end of something, especially when pointed; e.g. the sharp end of a pencil.
yap: noun. The high-pitched bark of a small dog, or similar.
acy: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cypriot Arabic.
apa: noun. A title used for male clergy or saints in the Coptic Church.
cpi: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Chinese Pidgin English.
pacay: noun. A Peruvian leguminous tree, Inga feuilleei, cultivated for its large white edible pods.
pata: noun. An Indian sword with an attached gauntlet.
pci: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Duruwa.
piccata: adjective. Sliced, sautéed and served with lemon, parsley and butter sauce.
pitaya: noun. Certain cactuses native to the Americas, of genera Selenicereus or Stenocereus, especially the latter.
taa: noun. A kind of pagoda in China and Japan.
tai: noun. A fish, the Japanese bream.
ticca: adjective. Engaged by the job, or on a contract.
yacca: noun. Either of two large evergreens of the West Indies, Podocarpus coriaceus and Podocarpus purdieanus.
yat: noun. A vowel letter of the Cyrillic and Glagolitic alphabet (Cyrillic capital Ѣ, Cyrillic small ѣ, Glagolitic ⱑ), no longer in current use
